name: React Component Patterns
description: Advanced React patterns for component composition, state management, and performance

compound_components.description: Components that work together to form a cohesive UI
compound_components.use_when: Building flexible, customizable components (tabs, select, accordion)
compound_components.implementation: Use React.Children or Context to share state

render_props.description: Pass function as children/prop to share logic
render_props.use_when: Need to share behavior with different rendering
render_props.still_useful: When component needs to own the DOM structure

higher_order_components.description: Function that takes component, returns enhanced component
higher_order_components.legacy: Mostly replaced by hooks, but still used in some libraries

best_practices: Follow hooks rules (top level, React functions only)

provider_pattern.description: Context API for sharing state down tree
provider_pattern.use_when: Data needed by many nested components (theme, auth, etc.)
provider_pattern.optimization: Use useMemo for context value to prevent re-renders

local_state.use_when: State only needed in component/small tree
local_state.prefer: Keep state as local as possible

lifted_state.description: State shared between siblings via common parent
lifted_state.pattern: Lift state to nearest common ancestor

url_state.description: State stored in URL (query params, hash)

global_state.recommendation: Start with Context/useState, add library when needed

server_state.description: Data from server (different from client state)

swr.use: Similar to React Query, lighter weight

rtk_query.use: Redux Toolkit's data fetching (if already using Redux)

server_state.best_practice: Separate server state from client state

react_memo.use: Prevent re-render if props haven't changed
react_memo.when: Expensive render or frequent parent re-renders
react_memo.note: Shallow comparison by default, can provide custom compare

use_callback.when: Passing callback to memoized child or useEffect dependency
use_callback.note: Only needed if child is memoized or in dependency array

virtualization.description: Render only visible items in long lists
virtualization.benefit: Massive performance improvement for long lists
virtualization.example:
{({ index, style }) =>
Row {index}
}

debounce.use: Wait for user to stop typing before action
debounce.example: Search input - wait 300ms after last keystroke
debounce.implementation: useDeferredValue or custom hook with setTimeout

use_transition.benefit: Keep UI responsive during heavy updates

use_deferred_value.benefit: Similar to useTransition, for values instead of updates

prefetching.example:
const queryClient = useQueryClient();
queryClient.prefetchQuery({ queryKey: ['post', id] });

error_boundary.description: Catch React errors in component tree
error_boundary.use: Wrap app sections to prevent full app crash
error_boundary.note: Only catches render errors, not async or event handlers

mocking.api_calls: Mock fetch with MSW (Mock Service Worker)

colocation.benefit: Easy to find everything for a component

best_practices: Keep components small and focused (Single Responsibility)
Memoize only when profiling shows benefit
Use proper semantic HTML and ARIA
Test user behavior, not implementation details
Use keys properly in lists (stable, unique, not index)
Avoid prop drilling with Context for deeply nested state
Split large components into smaller ones
Use custom hooks to extract logic
anti_patterns: Using index as key in dynamic lists
